description: The Jewish Community Council food pantry is normally on Thursdays, but the last Thursday was canceled due to snow and a late food delivery. As a result, they had a pantry on 'this Monday' starting at 9:30 AM until bags were distributed. There's a possible chance of another pantry on the 'Thursday of next week,' and people should call back on Wednesday to confirm. The pantry is small, distributing 350-400 bags. There are no ID requirements; IDs were waived and never reinstated. They offer groceries only, not refrigerated meals, including items like 1% milk, juice, rice, beans, canned corn, mixed vegetables, applesauce, and mixed fruit.
name: Jewish Community Council (JCC) of Pelham Parkway, Bronx

Only for students
A: Members may visit the Mav Market on any campus during weekly open hours. Upon arrival, our staff members will check you in (or sign you up if you are not already a member!) and tell you your weekly point allocation based on your family size. Each item within the Market has a point value, and you are free to shop for whatever items you need until you have reached your weekly point number. We operate on a trust system, so we do not count points or check bags. We recommend everyone brings their own reusable bag!
name: Mav Friendly Market

description_es: *Debe tener una aprobación previa para recoger alimentos* Envíe un correo electrónico a info@sapnanyc.org con su nombre completo, número de teléfono y dirección. Se comunicarán con usted con la solicitud (se puede completar por teléfono si hay barreras tecnológicas o de idioma). La solicitud tardará de 2 a 3 semanas en procesarse. Una vez aprobado, lo llamarán o le enviarán un mensaje de texto sobre cuándo puede visitarlo (generalmente cada dos viernes). Por lo general, se dan productos frescos, aceite de cocina, productos secos y huevos. Hablan bengalí, urdu y español y pueden ayudar con otros servicios sociales.
description: *Must be pre-approved to pick up food* Email info@sapnanyc.org with your full name, phone number, and address. They will get back to you with the application (can be completed over the phone if there are tech/language barriers). Application will take 2-3 weeks to process. Once approved, they will call/text you about when you can visit (usually every other Friday). Fresh produce, cooking oil, dry goods, and eggs are usually given. They speak Bangla, Urdu, and Spanish & can help with other social services.
name: SAPNA Food Pantry

The pantry has moved to the convent across the street. The previous building was sold and is now under construction. The new location is 2250 Ryer Avenue.
